page: Microsoft Azure L2s_v4 is an entry-level virtual machine within the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads (2 vCPUs) operating at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and includes 480 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. The CPU architecture is x86_64, incorporating advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The processor achieves strong single-core results, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2138.0 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.24 GB/sec. However, due to the physical constraint of having only a single physical core, multi-core scaling is limited. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated as poor, and the Geekbench multi-core score of 2622.0 is weak. Memory bandwidth also shows limitations, with uncached read bandwidth at 20.23 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 14.38 GB/sec.

This instance profile is tailored for workloads that require high single-threaded CPU clock speeds and local storage capacity but do not demand massive parallel processing. It is suitable for lightweight database operations, basic text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L2s_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and dedicated local storage at a lower entry point, though its resource density limits its utility for highly concurrent applications, multi-threaded software compilation, or large-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure L2s_v4 is a storage-capable virtual machine from the Lsv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, this instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2138.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak due to the single physical core constraint. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring high single-thread performance, moderate memory density, and local storage, such as small-scale database operations, text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks, while being less suited for highly parallelized applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L2s_v4 is an x86_64 server featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 480 GB local storage, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L2s_v4 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU, 16 GB RAM, and 480 GB local storage for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A single-core virtual machine featuring high clock speeds, dedicated local storage, and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 2 vCPUs
16.0 GB of system memory and 480 GB of local storage
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression
Limited multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth due to single physical core
Best suited for lightweight databases, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
